bundleModules: Core · Time Tracking · Time Off · Shifts · Performance · Trainings · Engagement
implementationNote: A DEFINIR NA DEMO — enterprise pricing for ~7,793 seats requires demo validation
pricingJustification: Planning PRO aligns with agreed demo focus on performance management before September cycle, plus trainings and engagement — modular entry without replacing SAP, UKG, or Payspace.
context: Story arc: Act I (2022 partial SAP promise), Act II (accountability without control — manual EPM, 1,000+ apps/vaga, 400+ managers), Act III (Malawi decentralizing from CENT — Tamanda as champion before Kumbo returns August). 7,793 employees, 3 sites, peak 10,000 seasonally.
priorities: Peak pain: loss of control + September deadline + Malawi ownership window. Phase 1 Performance, Phase 2 ATS, Phase 3 Trainings/Engagement. UKG/Payspace/SAP stay.
mobileStory: Mobile app for time off and shift management — relevant for factory site workers across Dwangwa and Nchalo
operationsStory: Seasonal sugar operations drive peak workforce to 10,000 — intense recruitment and data capture during season start
documentsStory: SAP SuccessFactors handles core employee data capture; performance reviews and EPM remain manual
recruitmentStory: 1,000+ applications per job advert — talent team evaluating ATS; Tamanda wished they had known Factorial 3–4 months ago
payrollStory: Payspace processes payroll — Factorial would sync employee data, not replace payroll processing
implementationStory: Modular phase-one entry on performance management; expand to recruitment if talent team shows interest after demo
nextSteps: WhatsApp coordination for demo date → Performance Management demo on Teams with Chido, Leila, Moses, BPs, training team → pricing and scope confirmed in demo → align before September review cycle
